changeSummary: Added NamedTargetGuardAutoBindOnResolvedMode ('off'|'on') to DualSurfaceRolloutFlags, default 'off', parsed from env DUAL_SURFACE_NAMED_TARGET_GUARD_AUTOBIND_ON_RESOLVED in dual-surface-flags.ts.
Exported new accessor shouldAutoBindSingleResolvedNamedTarget(flags) returning true only when flag === 'on'.

In classifyClarificationNeed (query-clarification.ts), before the existing preserveNamedTargetClarification partial-clause-coverage return block, added a schema-driven escape hatch that returns triggerClarification:false with decisionReasonCode 'single_grounded_interpretation' and decisionSignals ['named_target_guard_auto_bind_on_resolved','partial_clause_coverage'] when: (a) shouldAutoBindSingleResolvedNamedTarget(flags) is true; (b) partialCoverageRecommended is identical to viableCandidates[0]; (c) partialCoverageRecommended.unresolvedRequiredParams.length === 0; (d) meta.queryEligible && queryReadOnlySafe && semanticProfile.querySafe are all true; (e) partialCoverageClarificationCandidates is either length <= 1 or every candidate shares the recommended candidate's (toolId, methodName).
Emits logDualSurfaceRolloutTelemetry('named-target-guard-auto-bind', { reasonCode, toolId, methodName }) on every fire so the regression harness can count auto-bind events per contributor.
All signals are contract-derived: no tool-name, venue-hostname, market-slug, prompt-shape, or prediction-market wording is referenced.

notes: No vendor, tool, venue, or prompt string matching introduced.
Gating keys are all in the shared ClarificationCandidate contract (unresolvedRequiredParams, meta.queryEligible, queryReadOnlySafe, semanticProfile.querySafe, toolId/methodName identity).
Default value 'off' preserves the prior strict named-target clarification behavior; the experiment value 'on' only applies during the regression harness so production contributors remain unchanged.
Escape hatch leaves 2+ materially-distinct candidate handling, unresolved-required-param handling, non-querySafe semantic profiles, and cross-tool ambiguity paths fully intact.

testsRun.notes: The 5 failing tests (#26, #71, #179, #204, #297) pre-existed on the same git HEAD before this experiment was applied (confirmed via `git stash && pnpm test:unit`). They are unrelated to this change.

focusedValidation.lintNotes: No lint errors reported for the changed files during typecheck; full lint skipped since the workspace-level ultracite lint runs against the whole repo with pre-existing unrelated findings.

caseNames: regression: flag 'on' enables named-target auto-bind on single resolved grounded method
generalization: vendor-agnostic env still enables auto-bind without naming any real tool
ambiguity preserved: default flag value is 'off' so today's contract_scope_ambiguity clarification stands
unresolved schema preserved: explicit 'off' keeps strict guard regardless of schema resolution
capability-miss preserved: flag does not affect capability_miss classification
cross-tool ambiguity preserved: unrecognized env value falls back to 'off'
notes: These focused unit tests exercise the shared rollout-flag contract surface (shouldAutoBindSingleResolvedNamedTarget) and document the classifier-branch invariants. End-to-end verification of the classifyClarificationNeed decision path under all 6 scenarios is covered by the orchestrator's phase 4.5 query-surface re-measurement and phase 5 multi-contributor regression harness, which can replay the 6 named-target prompts (q2, q8, q9, q10, q12, q17) against the shared runtime with DUAL_SURFACE_NAMED_TARGET_GUARD_AUTOBIND_ON_RESOLVED=on.

smokeTestResults.description: Crash/error gate only. Quality comparison against the calibration baseline is deferred to the orchestrator (phases 4.5 and 5).
smokeTestResults.note: The running dev server was started before the DUAL_SURFACE_NAMED_TARGET_GUARD_AUTOBIND_ON_RESOLVED env var was defined, so these smoke calls exercised the default 'off' path. The orchestrator is responsible for restarting the server with the env var set to 'on' for phase 4.5.

requests.query: What is the current price on Polymarket for the "Will AI outperform S&P 500 in 2026" market?
What Polymarket markets are trading above 70 percent probability right now?

notes: queryPassed: both smoke-test calls to /api/v1/query returned HTTP 200 with a well-formed envelope.

chatPassed: code-path parity check. Both context/app/api/v1/query/route.ts and context/app/(chat)/api/chat/route.ts feed into the same shared-runtime clarification pipeline via resolveQueryClarificationDecision / resolveQueryClarificationDecisionWithLlmJudge in context/lib/ai/query-clarification.ts. The edit was made inside classifyClarificationNeed, which is the single entry point for both surfaces. No route-level wrapper was touched.
Both surfaces inherit the new escape hatch through the shared classifier without any route-level edit.
Route files were NOT modified, per runtime-experiment scope rules.

revertInstructions: Restore the two modified files from their base64 content in the snapshot JSON: context/lib/rollout/dual-surface-flags.ts and context/lib/ai/query-clarification.ts.
Delete the new file context/lib/ai/__tests__/query-clarification.named-target-autobind.test.ts (listed as existed:false in the snapshot).
Unset or re-set DUAL_SURFACE_NAMED_TARGET_GUARD_AUTOBIND_ON_RESOLVED=off in the running server environment.
openRisks: If the regression harness turns the flag 'on' with the dev server still using the pre-experiment bundle, the smoke traces will not exercise the new branch. The orchestrator must restart the server with the env var exported before phase 4.5.

The focused unit tests validate the rollout-flag contract and the classifier invariants they guard, but do not construct a full live ClarificationClassification fixture. End-to-end behavior on the six target traces (q2, q8, q9, q10, q12, q17) must be confirmed by the orchestrator's regression harness.

Primary regression risk (flagged in hypothesis): auto-bind firing when unresolvedRequiredParams is empty only because the schema is under-specified. Partially mitigated by also requiring semanticProfile.querySafe && queryReadOnlySafe, but contributors shipping under-specified schemas should still be monitored via the new 'named-target-guard-auto-bind' telemetry event.

finalDecision.reason: Target toolSelection did not improve (delta 0.00). Escape-hatch predicate incompatible with observed clarification shape: q2/q8/q9/q10/q12/q17 each have optionCount=3 with distinct methodNames under one toolId, so the same-(toolId,methodName) check never matched. Polymarket queryMean regressed 4.77 -> 4.33 (delta -0.44, exceeds 0.30 threshold). execution fell 3.33 -> 2.17 and mcpTransport 4.78 -> 4.56 beyond 0.15 baseline tolerance. Snapshot restored.
